topic: GitHub Model Selection Guide: Choosing Claude vs Codex for GitHub Coding Agents
keyword: github model selection claude codex agents

key_points: Model selection is now available for Claude and Codex agents on github.com (launched April 14, 2026)
Claude models available: Claude Sonnet 4.6, Claude Opus 4.6, Claude Sonnet 4.5, Claude Opus 4.5
Codex models available: GPT-5.2-Codex, GPT-5.3-Codex, GPT-5.4
Select a model when kicking off a task for access to latest and most capable models
GitHub Agent HQ launched February 4, 2026 as multi-agent command center
Run Claude, Codex, and GitHub Copilot on the same task simultaneously
Compare results side by side from different agents
Available in github.com, GitHub Mobile, and VS Code
Claude Code scores 87.6% on SWE-bench Verified; Codex leads Terminal-Bench 2.0 at 77.3%
Claude Code used 4x more tokens than Codex on identical benchmark tasks
Codex shipped subagents (up to 8 parallel agents) to GA on March 14, 2026
Claude Code's Agent Teams (research preview) supports coordinated multi-session tasks
Codex is cloud-native: runs in OpenAI's sandboxed environment asynchronously
Claude Code is terminal-first: runs locally, code never leaves your machine
Codex reads AGENTS.md open standard supported by thousands of open-source projects
Both agents require Copilot Business, Pro, Pro+, or Enterprise subscription
GitHub Copilot Auto model selection automatically picks best model for your prompts
Auto selects from GPT-4.1, GPT-5 mini, Claude Haiku 4.5, Claude Sonnet 4.5
Assign different agents to tasks that don't touch the same files to avoid merge conflicts
Multi-agent workflows can run architectural, logical, and implementation reviews in parallel

stats.fact: Claude Opus 4.7 scores 87.6% on SWE-bench Verified benchmark
Claude Code achieved 67% win rate in blind code quality evaluations over OpenAI Codex (early 2026)
Claude Code used 4x more tokens than Codex on identical benchmark tasks
Claude Opus 4.6 scores 80.8% and Claude Sonnet 4.6 scores 79.6% on SWE-bench Verified
GitHub Copilot Pro subscribers get 300 premium requests per month; Claude Opus 4.6 costs 3x multiplier (3 requests per session), Claude Sonnet 4.6 costs 1 request
GitHub Copilot pricing: Free tier, Pro $10/month, Pro+ $39/month, Business $19/user/month, Enterprise $39/user/month
Multi-agent workflows on Agent HQ cost 2–4x single-agent setups but typically justify productivity gains
Early Agent HQ users report errors occurring 5–10% of the time, requiring vigilant human oversight
Codex shipped subagents to GA on March 14, 2026, supporting up to 8 parallel agents simultaneously

angles: Model selection within agents: Sonnet vs Opus for Claude (cost vs quality tradeoff), different GPT versions for Codex
Task-based decision matrix: Claude for complex interactive/refactoring work, Codex for async/parallel cloud-native automation
Cost optimization: premium request multipliers explained for teams on Business/Enterprise plans
Multi-agent workflow: how to run Claude and Codex together in Agent HQ for better coverage
Enterprise vs individual developer setup: admin policies, repo settings, subscription requirements
AGENTS.md configuration standard: shared by Codex and open-source ecosystem, not yet adopted by Claude
Privacy and data residency: Claude Code runs locally (code stays on machine), Codex runs in OpenAI cloud sandbox
Auto model selection vs manual model selection: when to trust GitHub's AI picker vs override it
